name: Flamingo
summary: American flamingos are tall pink birds with long legs and curved beaks. They wade in shallow lagoons, filter tiny food from the water, and often stand gracefully on one leg.
metaTitle: Flamingo Facts | Habitat, Pink Color & Photos
metaDescription: Discover American flamingo facts with photos, lagoon habitat, filter feeding, chicks, and flocks. Learn why flamingos are pink and where they live.
searchIntents: why do flamingos stand on one leg

lifespan.captivity: Up to 50 years in human care

funFacts: Flamingos are pink because of pigments in the shrimp and algae they eat.
They feed with their heads upside down in the water.
Flamingo chicks hatch gray and turn pink as they grow.
Flamingos often stand on one leg to save body heat.
They nest in huge colonies on mud mounds.
American flamingos live in the Caribbean and Galápagos Islands.

faq.question: What is a flamingo?
faq.answer: A flamingo is a tall wading bird with pink feathers, long legs, and a curved beak. The American flamingo lives in the Caribbean and Galápagos.

faq.question: Where do flamingos live?
faq.answer: American flamingos live in shallow lagoons and coastal wetlands in the Caribbean, Galápagos Islands, and northern South America.

faq.question: What do flamingos eat?
faq.answer: Flamingos filter tiny brine shrimp, algae, and small animals from the water with their specialized beaks.

faq.question: Why are flamingos pink?
faq.answer: Natural pigments called carotenoids in their food build up in their feathers, turning them pink.

faq.question: Why do flamingos stand on one leg?
faq.answer: Standing on one leg may help flamingos keep warm by tucking one leg close to their body.

faq.question: How tall is a flamingo?
faq.answer: American flamingos stand about 4 to 5 feet tall, taller than most children.

faq.question: Are flamingos endangered?
faq.answer: American flamingos are listed as Least Concern, but habitat loss and pollution can threaten nesting colonies.
